text: 1. THOU, of a truth, O Prthivi, bearest the tool that rends the hills: Thou rich in torrents, who with might quickenest earth, O Mighty One. 2. To thee, O wanderer at will, ring out the lauds with beams of day, Who drivest, like a neighing steed, the swelling cloud, O bright of hue. 3. Who graspest with thy might on earth. e'en the strong sovrans of the wood, When from the lightning of thy cloud the rain-floods of the heaven descend.

metadata.title: Rig Veda - Mandala 5 Hymn 84
metadata.citation: Rig Veda, Mandala 5, Hymn 84; Ralph T. H. Griffith translation
